user_message: I need you to process this exec meeting notes from Humongous Insurance. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Humongous Insurance)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:10:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:14:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:18:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:23:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-N trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-N failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-N -- Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
